title: The World Reacts Before the Reverie
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4 as News of Whole Cake Island spreads across the world and the story pivots toward the Reverie.
scene: The world learns a distorted version of the Whole Cake Island incident as royal delegations move toward the Reverie.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Two years ago; in the summit war fought between the forces of the Marine HQ and the Whitebeard Pirates;

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Marines won but a pyrrhic victory. The battlefield, the marine base of marineford,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Marineford had long been the headquarters of justice, but, in light of the intersifying ten- signs of the pirate age, the world government

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: wished to rebuild it far more stead- fast than it had ever been.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A new Bastion of Justice, located at the entrance to the new world!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is Marine HQ, New Marineford, Led New Fleet Admiral Sakazuki!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and though its precise location may have changed, marine HQ was, as ever,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Doorguard for the Home of the celestial Dragons, the Holy Land of Mariejois.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I thought I said that until he brings me straw hat's and law's heads,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I refuse to allow him to cross a navy threshold!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How dare he bring that lousy face of his back here! That brat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Y... yes. You've mentioned this before, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Just drive him out, damn it?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Er, well... he has a perfect, logical defense, and... We can't say anything!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What kind of iron-headed dimmit are you?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Annoying, isn't it? Being fleet Admiral.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Keep yer trap shut, old man! yer half retired, anyway!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is the back side of the Grand Line Base G1,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Red Port. This enormous hair- borst raddles the red line,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And is a governmental route across the wall, to the ocean on the other side.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Here, one may get on the bubble lifts

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: That lead to the Holy Land, Mariejois:

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Look! There's more ships arriving now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Make way! Royals are coming through!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Why does he walk so slow?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: cheers, to the eyes of the world!()

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is better to die on your feet than live on your knees!

summary: This section advances the Reverie Setup storyline with 58 dialogue beats. The aftermath becomes world news and the narrative shifts toward the Reverie and global politics.
